exact.alert_type: Admin Consented to Unverified Enterprise App

exact._comment: Common noise for user file deletion actions in a shared mailbox or Teams environment. Unless we see this with other suspicious activity or from high-risk users, it's generally not actionable on its own.
Common noise for sending from a shared mailbox. Unless we see this with other suspicious activity or from high-risk users, it's generally not actionable on its own.

exact.alert_type: User Locked Out - Multiple Failed Logins
exact._comment: Common noise from Azure Smart-Lockout, which locks accounts after multiple failed login attempts. These can be caused by users forgetting passwords, mistyping, or automated attacks. Unless we see this with other suspicious activity or from high-risk users, it's generally not actionable on its own.
Non-Critical alert for users sharing files via anonymous links. Noise unless we see this with other suspicious activity or from high-risk users.

exact.alert_type: Login from New Device and IP
exact._comment: Simple a new device or IP address, no other suspicious activity, normal noise
Could not get compliance state for tenants due to missing licensing.
Most likely Standards API job runner noise, but could be other unknown noise. Needs investigation if we see this often or with high severity.
Normal VPP Token Expiry job runner noise.
Normal Token Refresh job runner noise.
Microsoft Notification of auto-remediation action taken, no action needed from team
External Email blocked for malware, no action needed from team

exact.alert_type: High-severity alert: Form blocked due to potential phishing attempt
exact._comment: Microsoft auto-remediated phishing form block. Same logic as Malware Campaign Detected

exact.alert_type: New vulnerabilities notification from Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
exact._comment: Microsoft notification of new vulnerabilities, no action needed from team

exact.alert_type: Microsoft 365 Defender has detected a security threat
exact._comment: Generic Defender notification email. These duplicate the specific alert emails (e.g. phishing, malware) that are already blacklisted above. Two identical copies of ID6952 came through 30 sec apart on 2026-03-26. If Defender finds something truly novel, it will come through Blackpoint or CIPP, not this generic mailbox notification.

contains.pattern: a user has logged on from a location you've set up to receive alerts for
You can view, comment on, or merge this pull request
important DSM update with critical fixes is available
Sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center to view your invoice.
Firewall has blocked a port scan
contains._comment: Bitdefender firewall port scan noise. FCOSM and Vicars Landing endpoints were generating 40+ of these per day on 2026-03-26, flooding Angela's dispatch queue.
Catches Bitdefender firewall alert email subjects. Belt-and-suspenders with the port scan rule above in case the message field contains the subject line.

Safety net for VPP Token Expiry alerts in case the exact alert_type match fails due to field extraction issues. These slipped through on 2026-03-26 for idenvironmental.com and umbrellaitgroup.com despite exact rule existing.
Vendor license delivery confirmations hitting the support mailbox. AI Triage was catching these but a classification rule is faster and deterministic.
Grandstream device management platform noise. AI Triage was catching these.
